records.title: Lucía Myrtea - plum
records.breeders: Centro de Edafología y Biología Aplicada del Segura-Consejo Superior de Investigaciones Científicas; Instituto Murciano de Investigación y Desarrollo Agrario y Alimentario, Spain; A. Guevara
records.inventors: Centro de Edafología y Biología Aplicada del Segura-Consejo Superior de Investigaciones Científicas; Instituto Murciano de Investigación y Desarrollo Agrario y Alimentario, Spain; A. Guevara
records.notes: Note: The first published version of this HortScience article, as well as other sources, represent this cultivar epithet as being trademarked, but that of course would violate Principle 6 of the International Code of Nomenclature for Cultivated Plants, p. 4 : “trademarks are the intellectual property of an individual or corporate body and are not therefore freely available for any person to use; consequently, they cannot be considered as names as defined in this Code.” Note that this name is properly spelled 'Lucía Myrtea', not 'lucía myrtea' as in the HortScience article; the correct name is given in the Official Gazette of the CPVO 2021-1, dated 2021-02-15; also in Ruiz, D., et al. "New promising Japanese plum cultivars for warm areas from CEBAS-CSIC/IMIDA breeding programme", in ActaHort 1322. In addition see ICNCP 21.3, p. 24: "Each word of a cultivar epithet must start with an initial capital letter unless linguistic custom demands otherwise."

records.title: Victoria Myrtea - plum
records.breeders: Centro de Edafología y Biología Aplicada del Segura-Consejo Superior de Investigaciones Científicas; Instituto Murciano de Investigación y Desarrollo Agrario y Alimentario, Spain; A. Guevara
records.inventors: Centro de Edafología y Biología Aplicada del Segura-Consejo Superior de Investigaciones Científicas; Instituto Murciano de Investigación y Desarrollo Agrario y Alimentario, Spain; A. Guevara
records.notes: Note: The original (first published online) version of the HortScience article and other sources represent this cultivar epithet as being trademarked, but that of course would violate Principle 6 of the International Code of Nomenclature for Cultivated Plants (ICNCP), p. 4 : “trademarks are the intellectual property of an individual or corporate body and are not therefore freely available for any person to use; consequently, they cannot be considered as names as defined in this Code.” Note that this name is properly written 'Victoria Myrtea', not 'victoria myrtea' as in the HortScience article; the correct name is given in the Official Gazette of the CPVO 2021-1, dated 2021-02-15. Also see ICNCP 21.3, p. 24: "Each word of a cultivar epithet must start with an initial capital letter unless linguistic custom demands otherwise."
